summary: Frontend-only Expo web prototype tested at 390x844 mobile viewport. Home, Allocation, Compare, Confirm, Success, Rate-Alert, Tools (Calculator/Ladder/Liquidity), bottom tabs (Home/Passbook/Explore/Profile), banner dismiss, and Source Modal all render and navigate correctly. Slider propagation verified end-to-end via pointer drag: setting safe split to 60% correctly carries ₹60,000 into /compare and /confirm, and the arithmetic is mathematically correct (₹60,000 × 8.10% = ₹4,860 interest, − ₹972 tax = ₹3,888 net). Banks on /compare sort by rate / safety / tax with Unity (current bank) always pinned last. Booking with default 70% adds ₹70,000 → Home invested updates from ₹25,000 → ₹95,000 (₹25k + ₹70k). One real bug found: tapping the source chip inside the notification dropdown navigates to /rate-alert instead of opening the source modal (nested Pressable click bubbles to parent). Several React-Native-Web console warnings present (shadow*, pointerEvents, transform-origin) — non-blocking but worth main agent's attention.

ui_bugs.issue: Tapping the 'RBI MPC statement' source chip inside the notification item (testID 'notif-source') does NOT open the Source Modal. The tap is captured by the parent Pressable (testID 'notif-item') whose onPress closes the dropdown and routes to /rate-alert. As a result, the source modal never appears for this chip — the user has no way to inspect the RBI source from the notification. Reference: /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/index.tsx:71-101 (SourceChip nested inside Pressable with router.push).

issues: Console warning: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' — coming from theme.ts shadow.soft / shadow.card used across all cards.
Console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' — likely from the Slider track/thumb.
Console error: 'Invalid DOM property transform-origin. Did you mean transformOrigin' — likely from the Donut/SVG component on /allocation.

action_items: Fix notification source chip swallowing: stop click propagation when the SourceChip inside the notif-item is pressed, or restructure so the Pressable is not a child of another Pressable. E.g. in /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/index.tsx, replace the outer Pressable with a TouchableOpacity that has a non-pressable inner View around the chip, and put the chevron/navigate action on a separate button.
Migrate StyleSheet shadow* props to boxShadow (or wrap in Platform.select) in /app/frontend/src/theme.ts to silence the react-native-web deprecation warning.
In /app/frontend/src/Donut.tsx, use camelCase `transformOrigin` instead of `transform-origin` (likely an SVG-style attribute issue).
Optional: also add explicit `testID="source-modal-backdrop"` tap behavior test passes already, no change needed.
critical_code_review_comments: store.tsx is in-memory only (Context + useState) — state resets on full reload. Acceptable for a prototype but worth flagging.

Slider (react-native-community/slider) on web renders as role="slider" div without an inner <input type=range>, so it does not respond to keyboard arrow keys or programmatic value injection. UX works (pointer drag), but accessibility is limited.

Nested <Pressable> inside another <Pressable> is used in (tabs)/index.tsx notif dropdown — same pattern works elsewhere because SourceChips are not inside another tap target, but here it causes the bug above.
Banner dismiss state lives in StableMoneyContext and persists for the session only (no AsyncStorage) — matches spec ('stays dismissed for the session').
seed_data_creation: None — fully self-contained seeded prototype (no backend, no MongoDB).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Mobile viewport 390x844. Slider is a div with role=slider; only pointer drag works (use bounding_box → mouse.down/move/up). Bottom tabs are reachable via page.get_by_role('tab', name='<Label>'). After the notif-source bug is fixed, re-verify that (a) tapping 'notif-source' opens the Source Modal (testID 'source-modal') with title 'RBI Monetary Policy statement' or similar, and (b) tapping the rest of the notif-item still navigates to /rate-alert. All other flows passed and do not need re-test unless related code is touched.
rca_of_issue: Reproduction: Open app → tap bell (testID notif-bell) → tap 'RBI MPC statement' chip (testID notif-source). Expected: Source Modal opens. Actual: dropdown closes and app navigates to /rate-alert. Root cause: The SourceChip is rendered as a <Pressable> child of the notif-item <Pressable> in /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/index.tsx. In react-native-web (and RN), Pressable does not stop event propagation by default, so the parent's onPress (setNotifOpen(false); router.push('/rate-alert')) fires regardless of which inner child was actually tapped, overriding the inner openSource() call (or racing with the navigation that unmounts the dropdown before the modal can show). Mitigation: (1) Replace the parent Pressable with a tappable surface that has a dedicated chevron button on the right, and leave the source chip independent; or (2) intercept the chip's onPress with a stopPropagation handler in web, or use TouchableWithoutFeedback wrapping. The cleanest fix is option (1).
